name: El Barranco del Agua y el Cinto Pedro — circular en Jalance
region_intro: Hiking around Jalance offers access to a diverse natural landscape characterized by river canyons, mountainous elevations, and Mediterranean forests. The region, situated in the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes, features varied terrain from dramatic river valleys to higher "muelas" (table mountains). Hikers can explore paths carved by the Júcar River and ascend to viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. This area provides a range of outdoor experiences for different ability levels. Best hiking trails around Jalance The most popular hiking route is Los Canyones del Jucar — Tour from Jalance, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route guides hikers through the dramatic Júcar River canyons. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cañón del Júcar — circular desde Jalance, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This circular route offers views of the Júcar River and its surrounding canyon landscape. Local hikers also love the El Barranco del Agua y el Cinto Pedro — circular en Jalance, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through the Barranco del Agua, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Jalance is defined by river canyons, mountainous terrain, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Jalance are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 150 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Jalance's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Jalance, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Jalance offers a diverse network of over 18 hiking trails. You'll find options for various skill levels, including 6 easy routes, 11 moderate trails, and 1 more challenging hike, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Jalance?
A: Yes, Jalance features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130400475/canon-del-jucar-circular-desde-jalance" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón del Júcar — circular desde Jalance&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views of the river canyon. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130562201/el-barranco-del-agua-y-el-cinto-pedro-circular-en-jalance" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barranco del Agua y el Cinto Pedro — circular en Jalance&lt;/a&gt;, leading through the Barranco del Agua.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Jalance?
A: Yes, Jalance has several easy trails suitable for families. These routes often feature gentle terrain and offer opportunities to enjoy nature without strenuous climbs. Look for trails marked as 'easy' in the komoot guide for suitable options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Jalance?
A: Many trails in Jalance are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural features to discover while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its dramatic natural features. You can discover the breathtaking Júcar River canyons, with towering walls and lush Mediterranean forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822749535/los-canyones-del-jucar-tour-from-jalance" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Canyones del Jucar — Tour from Jalance&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent way to experience these. Additionally, the El Corbinet Waterfall is a true paradise, especially in warmer weather, where bathing is permitted in its crystal-clear waters.

Q: What historical sites or caves can I explore on hikes near Jalance?
A: Jalance offers a rich blend of natural and historical attractions. You can hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2751423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which, though unrestored, provides incredible views of the Júcar River. The Tejador Cave near the Cortes stream offers a picturesque setting, and the Abrigo de las Monteses, a UNESCO World Heritage site, houses ancient Levantine rock art.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Jalance?
A: The best time to hike in Jalance is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be hot, but shaded trails and spots like El Corbinet Waterfall offer refreshing breaks. Winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some higher routes might be more exposed.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Jalance?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near the main trailheads and popular starting points for hikes in and around Jalance. Specific parking information can often be found in the details of individual komoot tours.

Q: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect on the trails?
A: Hikers in Jalance can expect diverse and stunning views. Trails often lead through the dramatic Júcar River canyons, offering panoramic vistas of the river and surrounding landscapes. You'll also encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1772531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar River and Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and historical landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jalance Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Jalance?
A: The trails in Jalance are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from nearly 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning river canyon views, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Jalance?
A: Yes, Jalance offers 6 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. These trails typically have minimal elevation gain and well-maintained paths, making them accessible for all fitness levels. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Workers' settlement – Peña del Buitre loop from El Sapo y la Sapa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there shaded trails for hiking in Jalance during summer?
A: While summer in Jalance can be warm, many trails pass through lush Mediterranean forests and along riverbanks, offering natural shade. The Júcar River canyons, with their deep ravines, also provide cooler microclimates. It's advisable to start early in the morning during summer months to avoid the midday heat.

You have to cross steps made with stones in the water. When passing a curve you can see the remains of the Amadeo mill.
